seo.meta_title: Logto vs WorkOS for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Choosing between Logto and WorkOS? We break down the differences in pricing, setup, and Next.js integration to help you decide.
seo.og_title: Logto vs WorkOS: Next.js Auth Comparison (2026)
seo.og_description: Logto scores 7/10 for Next.js DX. WorkOS scores 5/10. See how they compare on pricing, features, and setup time.

hero.headline: Logto vs WorkOS for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Logto and WorkOS — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Both Logto and WorkOS are solid choices for Next.js authentication, but they serve different needs. Logto edges ahead with a stronger Next.js integration score (7/10 vs 5/10). Where WorkOS shines is No free tier (enterprise sales). If you are optimizing for developer experience in the App Router, Logto is the safer bet.
verdict_text: Logto is our pick for this matchup. The 7/10 Next.js score reflects how well it integrates with server components, middleware, and edge runtime. WorkOS is not far behind at 5/10, but Logto's developer experience gives it the edge for most projects.

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

faq.question: Should I choose Logto or WorkOS for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Logto scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. WorkOS scores 5/10 with a Advanced learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Logto because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, up to 5,000 MAU or No free tier (enterprise sales) is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Logto or WorkOS more affordable?
faq.answer: Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Logto or WorkOS easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Logto is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Logto and WorkOS together?
faq.answer: Using both Logto and WorkOS in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Logto or WorkOS?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.1★. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, 5/10, and 4★. On paper, Logto leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
